title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During an interrogation, Elizabeth Whitaker, a mathematics and Latin teacher, tells Hercule Poirot that while she did not know Joyce Reynolds well, she remembers seeing the girl acting clumsily while cutting flour during the party games. When Poirot asks about a former teacher named Janet White, Whitaker becomes visibly unsettled and questions if the matter is related to the current investigation. She reveals that Janet White was strangled one evening while walking through a wood near a mining area. Poirot notes that Janet and Nora Ambrose were close friends and both were considered "too free." Whitaker explains that following the unsolved murder, Nora left school to work in the north of England.

Poirot later visits Stonehaven House, where an elderly woman informs him that the Colonel and Mrs. Weston are currently in London. He proceeds to Stonehaven Wood to visit the Quarry Garden, an underground garden created from a derelict quarry by a gardener named Michael Garfield. As Poirot admires the meticulously arranged autumnal landscape, he reflects on the potential connections between a forged will, a missing girl, and the creation of the garden. He contemplates whether the garden's design reflects the specific vision of his patron, Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith, or the artistic execution of Michael Garfield, noting that the garden appears to grow freely despite being strictly planned.
